name: hr.i9.compliance
description: Draft careful HR/I-9/E-Verify guidance and approval-gated email responses without giving legal advice or bypassing approvals.

message.description: HR, I-9, E-Verify, or work-authorization message to analyze or draft around.

examples: draft a response to this I-9 email
what should I do with this E-Verify notice?
turn this HR compliance email into next steps
does this work authorization message need action?
purpose: Guide Form I-9, E-Verify, reverification, document review, receipt, TNC, and HR compliance messaging with cautious non-legal language and approval-gated drafts.
whenToUse: A request mentions Form I-9, I-9 Section 1, Section 2, Section 3, E-Verify, reverification, TNC, employment eligibility, document review, receipts, or HR onboarding compliance.
The user needs a safe next-step checklist for I-9 deadlines, late completion, correction, remote document review, or reverification.
The user asks for a draft HR response about acceptable documents, I-797A/C, I-94, foreign passport, EAD, Permanent Resident Card, or Social Security card restrictions.
The request asks whether an I-9 or E-Verify notice needs action without enough facts to give a final compliance answer.
The agent needs to warn that employees choose which acceptable documents to present.
whenNotToUse: Do not use as the primary skill for general writing polish with no HR, I-9, E-Verify, or employment eligibility issue.
Do not use for student CPT/OPT eligibility unless the issue is also an employer I-9, E-Verify, or onboarding document question.
Do not use to provide legal advice, final USCIS interpretations, or instructions to discriminate based on citizenship or immigration status.
Do not use to send an email, submit E-Verify, alter an HR record, or create a calendar/task without the existing approval flow.
negativeSignals: choose which document the employee must present
immigration strategy unrelated to employer verification
requiredContext: Whether this is Section 1, Section 2, Section 3, E-Verify, reverification, correction, receipt, TNC, or remote review.
Known dates: hire date, first day of employment, notice date, deadline, reverification date, or late-completion date.
The exact HR message or facts provided, without inventing immigration status or document details.
Whether the user is asking for analysis, a checklist, or a draft response.
missingContextQuestions: Which I-9 or E-Verify step is involved: Section 1, Section 2, Section 3, reverification, correction, receipt, TNC, or remote review?
What exact deadline or date is shown in the HR notice?
What facts are already known from the employee or HR message?
Are you asking for a safe checklist only, or a draft response that should stay approval-gated?

outputContract.format: Issue -> Known facts -> Missing facts -> Deadline/risk check -> Safe next step -> Approval-gated draft if requested.
mustInclude: A clear statement that this is not legal advice.
A missing facts checklist when the request is incomplete.
The employee-choice-of-documents rule when documents are discussed.
Deadline identification or a note that the deadline is unknown.
Approval-gated status for any outbound HR draft.
mustAvoid: Do not tell an employee which acceptable document to present.
Do not request unnecessary sensitive documents.
Do not invent immigration, identity, or employment authorization facts.
Do not auto-send emails or modify HR/E-Verify records.
Do not make final legal conclusions.

safetyRules: Use safe, non-legal language and state uncertainty when facts are missing.
Do not tell an employee which document to present for I-9; employees choose from acceptable documents.
Do not ask for extra documents beyond what is necessary for the stated I-9 or E-Verify process.
Outbound HR messages are drafts only and require ApprovalAction before sending.
Do not submit E-Verify, edit HR records, create calendar events, or write files from skill guidance.
Do not expose secrets, credentials, or raw private account data.
approvalRequiredFor: Sending or replying to HR email
Creating or updating an email draft
Changing HR, onboarding, I-9, or E-Verify records
positiveExamples: I need to ask an employee for their I-9 document.
What should I do with this E-Verify TNC notice?
Draft a safe HR reply about an I-797A and I-94 for I-9.
The new hire completed Section 1 late. What should HR say?
Can a receipt be used for Section 2 and what deadline matters?
Make this I-9 email to HR sound less robotic.
negativeExamples: Make this lease email sound warmer.
Score this GRC analyst internship for me.
Write a VPN troubleshooting ticket note.

evaluationPrompts.input: I need to ask an employee for their I-9 document.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Positive I-9 document request; must warn that the employee chooses documents.

evaluationPrompts.input: What should I do with this E-Verify tentative nonconfirmation notice?
Draft a safe HR reply about an I-797A and I-94 for a new hire I-9.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Positive I-9 document and draft request with approval gate.

evaluationPrompts.input: The hire completed Section 1 late and HR wants a correction note.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Positive Section 1 late completion and correction.

evaluationPrompts.input: Can we use a receipt for Section 2 remote document review?
evaluationPrompts.reason: Positive receipt and remote review workflow.

evaluationPrompts.input: Make this lease email sound friendlier.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Negative general writing request with no I-9 or HR compliance issue.

evaluationPrompts.input: Score this GRC internship against my Security+ background.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Negative career screening belongs to GRC and job application skills.

evaluationPrompts.input: Write a ticket note for VPN not connecting.
This I-9 document looks off. Is it okay?
evaluationPrompts.reason: Ambiguous I-9 document review; should match but ask for missing facts.

evaluationPrompts.input: HR says there is an E-Verify deadline but I only have this screenshot.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Ambiguous E-Verify deadline; should flag missing exact date and notice text.

evaluationPrompts.input: Make this I-9 email to HR sound less robotic.
evaluationPrompts.reason: Multi-skill: I-9 should pair with Writing Humanizer for tone.
